summary: Provision and operate Amazon GuardDuty. Enabling the account detector is an engineer-gated create form; disabling detection or its data sources is a security-posture change that never auto-applies.

operations.title: Enable GuardDuty
operations.summary: Create the account GuardDuty detector, enabled. Disabling it or its data sources blinds threat detection.
operations.description: Create an aws_guardduty_detector, enabled, for the account. Data sources (S3 protection, malware scanning, Kubernetes audit logs) are enabled as a reviewed follow-up; this turns detection on. Submitting drafts the resource and routes it to an engineer to review and finish.

params.name: detector_label
params.label: Detector label
params.help: A short label naming the Terraform block only; the detector has no name attribute in AWS.

params.name: enable
params.label: Enable
params.help: The detector is created enabled; written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: finding_publishing_frequency
params.label: Finding frequency
params.help: How often finding updates are published to the account.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: Description
params.help: A short description recorded as the resource's Description tag.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: The team or person accountable for this resource, recorded as its PIC tag.

decisions: Disabling the detector or suspending its data sources blinds threat detection for the whole account; enablement and scope changes are engineer-reviewed, never self-service.
Data sources (S3 protection, malware scanning, Kubernetes audit logs) are enabled as a reviewed follow-up; this provisions the detector enabled with none added yet.
